---
title: "Liam Trethowan"
navbar:
     title: false
     logo: 'GEoM_logo.png'
     left:
      - about.qmd
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile2.png


---

I am a Research Leader at Herbarium Kew - building collaborative solutions for interesting problems. My focus is simple - the ecology and geography of megadiversity and how this knowledge can boost conservation. My work often develops hierarchical joint species distribution/abundance models to understand how evolution, traits and environment shape communities. Active personal and career development of people is a big part of what I think about - making the team my priority and our work together the most rewarding part of my day-to-day.  

## Contact

l.trethowan@kew.org  
liamtrethowan@gmail.com  

## Editing

I am an associate editor for the Journal of Tropical Ecology and review for journals including Conservation Biology, Journal of Ecology, Journal of Biogeography, Ecography and Reinwardtia.  

## Education

Manchester Metropolitan University x
RBG Kew | 
PhD Forest Ecology of Sulawesi | Sept 2015 - Oct 2018  

University of Leeds |   
1st class BSc Ecology (Industrial year) | Sept 2011 - June 2015

## Experience

RBG Kew | Research Leader | August 2024 - present

RBG Kew | Postdoctoral Research Associate - West Papuan spatial prioritisation | April 2022 - July 2024

RBG Kew | Postdoctoral Research Associate - Wallacean green economy | April 2019 - April 2022
